data.title: Ukraine considers shift from dollar to euro amid geopolitical realignments
data.description:
Ukraine is starting to consider a shift away from the U.S. dollar, possibly linking its currency more closely to the euro amid the splintering of global trade and its growing ties to Europe, Central Bank Governor Andriy Pyshnyi told Reuters.

Potential accession to the European Union, a "strengthening of the EU’s role in ensuring our defense capabilities, greater volatility in global markets, and the probability of global-trade fragmentation," are forcing the central bank to review whether the euro should be the reference currency for Ukraine’s hryvnia instead of the dollar, Pyshnyi said in emailed remarks.

"This work is complex and requires high-quality, versatile preparation," Pyshnyi added, in the most direct comments by a Ukrainian official on a possible shift.

The dollar dominates international trade and accounts for the majority of global reserves. Major economies including Saudi Arabia and Hong Kong peg their currencies to the dollar.

But under President Donald Trump, the U.S. has unleashed a trade war by introducing what could be the highest tariffs in a century, a move that has prompted some observers to question the future role of the dollar as global reserve currency.

Now in the fourth year of fighting an invasion by Russia, Ukraine has also seen Trump temporarily cut off some military assistance to the country.

European leaders, including from the EU, have vowed to strengthen Kyiv’s army to ensure it can be the cornerstone of future security in Ukraine but progress has been difficult.

Meanwhile, Ukraine struck an agreement that gives the United States preferential access to new Ukrainian mineral deals and which funds investment in the nation’s reconstruction.

Since Trump’s return to the White House, the greenback is down more than 9% against a basket of major currencies as investors pull back from owning U.S. assets.

Some experts warn against associating the strength of the dollar to its reserve-currency status. Yet historically, dollar holdings have been linked to security alliances and military ties to Washington.

Transactions with the U.S. dollar continue to dominate all segments of the FX market, said Pyshnyi, but the share of euro-denominated transactions has been rising in most segments though ”so far moderately.” He did not elaborate.

Ukraine launched the hryvnia in 1996, and over the decades it has used the dollar as the reference currency.

Immediately after Russia’s invasion in February 2022, the central bank imposed capital controls and pegged the hryvnia at an official rate of about 29 to the U.S. dollar. Ukraine was forced to devalue later due to a buildup of fiscal imbalances.

In October 2023, the central bank moved from a firm peg to a managed exchange-rate regime that uses the U.S. dollar as the reference – the gauge to measure FX interventions and for smoothing fluctuations in the exchange rate.

The EU opened membership talks with Ukraine and Moldova nearly a year ago, although a long and tough road lies ahead before it could join the bloc. EU President Ursula von der Leyen said in February that Ukraine could be in by 2030 provided it continued to enact reforms to its political and judicial system at the current pace.

In preparation, Moldova switched its reference currency for the Moldovan lei to the euro from the dollar on Jan. 2.

A revival of investment and consumer activity thanks to closer links with Europe and economic normalisation would help economic growth pick up slightly over the next two years to 3.7-3.9%, Pyshnyi said, though much of the economic trajectory depends on how the conflict develops.

”A quick end to the war would clearly be a positive scenario with good economic outcomes if it were to incorporate security guarantees for Ukraine,” Pyshnyi said.

”Nevertheless, it’s crucial to acknowledge that the economic benefits of ending the war would likely take time to fully materialize.”

Ukraine is relying on external financing to help fund the war effort. Pyshnyi said he expected $55 billion this year which would not only cover the budget deficit but also be used to set aside a public-finance reserve for the coming years, when aid volumes were likely to start declining.”We project Ukraine will receive about $17 billion in 2026 and $15 billion in 2027,” said Pyshnyi.

data.title: Boeing proposes 2027 delivery date for new Air Force One jets, official says
data.description:
Boeing has proposed delivering its delayed new version of Air Force One in 2027, a U.S. Air Force official said on Wednesday.

President Donald Trump negotiated with Boeing for a pair of new 747-8 aircraft during his 2017-2021 first administration. They were initially expected to be delivered in 2024.

"We are looking at the requirements that are being potentially traded off to get to that date, and so I would not necessarily guarantee that date, but they're proposing to bring it into '27," Darlene Costello, acting assistant secretary of the Air Force, told the House Armed Services Committee.

Costello said the Air Force is working with the White House on "what's acceptable" from a capability standpoint for Air Force One.

The government has temporarily lifted some security requirements for the production facility, Costello said. This, she said, has allowed Boeing to be more "efficient and productive" in assembling the aircraft.

She said there are a few remaining issues that need to be resolved but once they are, it will give a better estimate on the schedule of delivery.

Boeing deferred questions to the Air Force.

In mid-February, Trump expressed his displeasure with Boeing but ruled out European rival Airbus as an alternative. Around the same time, Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg said Elon Musk, who serves as an adviser to Trump, was "helping us a lot" in navigating delivery of the presidential jet.

The government recently tapped defense contractor L3Harris Technologies to overhaul a Boeing 747 formerly used by the Qatari government for use as an interim Air Force One amid Boeing's delays.

That aircraft is expected to be available for use as early as fall, according to The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: PDD Holdings' Temu Likely to Face Challenges Amid Tariff TensionsMarket Talk
data.description: PDD Holdings' Temu is likely to face challenges in the coming months amid tariff tensions, according to Citi analysts in a research note. Citi has observed a notable decline in sales on Temu's site, the analysts note. "Together with Temu's decision to also halt all goods that shipped directly from China...we believe Temu will gradually lose price competitiveness it used to enjoy and suffer further revenue decline from the U.S.," they say. Noting that this move wasn't unexpected, the analysts say that this is still a major setback to the company's global expansion ambition. "With more countries likely to scrutinize the imported goods of small parcels, Temu could face more sales headwinds in coming months," the analysts say. The analysts maintain a neutral/high risk on PDD Holdings, with a target price of its ADRs of US$127.00. Its ADRs were last traded at US$109.39. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0313 GMT - PDD Holdings' Temu is likely to face challenges in the coming months amid tariff tensions, according to Citi analysts in a research note. Citi has observed a notable decline in sales on Temu's site, the analysts note. "Together with Temu's decision to also halt all goods that shipped directly from China...we believe Temu will gradually lose price competitiveness it used to enjoy and suffer further revenue decline from the U.S.," they say. Noting that this move wasn't unexpected, the analysts say that this is still a major setback to the company's global expansion ambition. "With more countries likely to scrutinize the imported goods of small parcels, Temu could face more sales headwinds in coming months," the analysts say. The analysts maintain a neutral/high risk on PDD Holdings, with a target price of its ADRs of US$127.00. Its ADRs were last traded at US$109.39. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0300 GMT - Amata Corp.'s stock is likely a deep-value play given the share price's sharp year-to-date correction, Thanachart Securities' Rata Limsuthiwanpoom says in a research report. The stock is also trading at a steep 31% discount to its land value, where the brokerage uses highly conservative assumptions to calculate U.S. tariff risks. The stock price is at its pandemic-crisis low of 0.8X price-to-book-value, the analyst says. The brokerage keeps a buy rating but raises the stock's target price to THB23.00 from THB20.00 to partly reflect its upwardly revised discount for the industrial estate provider's developed and raw land. Shares closed 3.3% higher at THB15.80 on Wednesday. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0219 GMT - Malaysia's tech sector could see a boost in sentiment if the U.S. eases AI chip export curbs, Kenanga IB analyst Cheow Ming Liang says in a note. The Trump administration plans to rescind and revise Biden-era restrictions, potentially easing curbs on advanced chip sales. Cheow sees this as a near-term catalyst for NationGate amid recovering demand and potential clarity in policies. Kenanga maintains a neutral rating on Malaysia's tech sector, urging a trading-focused strategy, citing persistent volatility, and names Kelington Group as its top pick. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0219 GMT - Cathay Pacific Airways' profitability is poised to gain from falling fuel costs, three members of HSBC Global Research say in a research report. Singapore jet fuel price has fallen 19% on year so far in 2Q, the members note. The HSBC oil and gas team forecasts Brent crude oil to average $68.50/bbl in 2025, down 14% from 2024, and projects a further drop to $65/bbl in 2026. Going forward, structural oversupply in the global oil market, exacerbated by OPEC+ production increase, positions airlines to benefit from prolonged fuel price stability. HSBC lifts its 2025-2027 recurring profit estimates for Cathay Pacific by 7%. It raises the stock's rating to hold from reduce and the target price to HK$9.40 from HK$9.20. Shares are 0.1% higher at HK$9.31. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0213 GMT - CelcomDigi's outlook may remain positive, supported by stronger merger synergies and a potential rerating once network integration is completed in 2H, delays notwithstanding, RHB IB's Jeffrey Tan says in a note. While IT upgrades and higher 5G wholesale costs may lift operating expenditure in 2025, the telecom company targets 700 million ringgit-800 million ringgit in pretax postmerger synergies by 2027, the analyst notes. RHB raises CelcomDigi's target price to MYR4.40 from MYR4.30 while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 1.3% lower at MYR3.86. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0151 GMT - SD Guthrie's strong 1Q performance is unlikely to be sustained, with earnings expected to moderate in the coming quarters due to weaker crude palm oil prices, slower global growth, and subdued downstream margins, say CIMB Securities analysts. The planter has guided for lower production costs and stronger fresh fruit bunch output, but near-term catalysts remain limited, say Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia in a note. The analysts expect CPO prices to trend lower in 2Q and 3Q, and caution that U.S. reciprocal tariffs may dampen palm oil demand and delay SD Guthrie's land monetization plans. CIMB downgrades SD Guthrie's rating to hold from buy and cuts target price to MYR5.06 from MYR5.50. Shares are up 0.6% at MYR4.59. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0134 GMT - Malaysia's rubber-product sector may post weaker 2Q earnings due to sluggish sales, soft average selling prices and a stronger ringgit, says Affin Hwang IB analyst Andrew Lim in a note. Earlier expectations had been for a strong sales recovery from April, but 2Q sales volumes are now expected to rise just 5%-10% on quarter, below earlier forecasts, he notes. Aggressive price cuts by Chinese rivals continue to pressure average selling prices, he says. Despite tariffs imposed on China-made medical gloves since January, demand from the U.S. for Malaysia-made gloves may remain lackluster, he adds. Affin Hwang maintains a neutral rating on Malaysia's rubber-product sector, rating Kossan Rubber Industries as its preferred pick due to solid fundamentals. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0129 GMT - Aggregate Taiwan corporate earnings may be negatively impacted by up to 6% "assuming the TWD stays at its current level," says Goldman Sachs' Portfolio Strategy team. The USD/TWD has currently stabilized around 30.2-30.3, the team notes. The negative impact will probably be contributed by exporters and life insurers, and offset by modest benefits from lower USD-based imports, the team says in a research report. GS maintains its underweight market stance on Taiwan's equities and keeps its 10% EPS growth forecast for MSCI Taiwan this year. GS continues to favor defensives, domestic, and high-dividend stocks in Taiwan. USD/TWD is down 0.1% at 30.38. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0117 GMT - ANZ's improved asset quality helps offset the disappointment of the lender's slight first-half revenue miss, Citi analyst Thomas Strong says. He tells clients in a note that ANZ's performance over the six months through March looks thematically similar to rival National Australia Bank, with better asset quality and improved costs. Strong thinks that investors will continue to focus on ANZ's costs and strategy ahead of its impending change of management and regulatory oversight. Citi has a last-published neutral rating and A$27.50 target price on the stock, which is down 2.4% at A$29.27. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0107 GMT - SD Guthrie is expected to post weaker earnings ahead after a strong 1Q result, as crude palm oil prices have dropped 18% year to date, Citi says in a note. Despite robust upstream gains supported by higher CPO prices and improved labor efficiency, analyst Huan Wen Gan sees limited upside going forward. Gan warns that coming seasonal production increases and a weak crude oil price, affecting the palm oil-gas oil spread, will pressure CPO prices further. Citi maintains a sell call on SD Guthrie and keeps its target price at MYR3.90. Shares are 1.7% higher at MYR4.64. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0049 GMT - Briscoe's 1Q trading update was a mixed bag for Forsyth Barr. Sales were slightly softer than analyst Paul Laxton Koraua had expected, but half-year net profit guidance of NZ$30 million implies a better first-half gross margin outlook than thought. Forsyth Barr notes that Briscoe is committed to maintaining the same level of profitability as last year, which signals a seasonal recovery through 2H. "The next key news flow will be Briscoe's annual shareholder meeting on May 15, where the company typically provides a more fulsome update," Forsyth Barr says. "On our revised earnings, Briscoe is trading on circa 14x 12-month forward PE--slightly above historic levels." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

0048 GMT - What Infratil decides to do with a 10% stake in Contact Energy will be interesting, says Forsyth Barr. Infratil will have the equity interest, currently worth around NZ$900 million, once Contact's takeover of Manawa Energy completes. Analyst Aaron Ibbotson doesn't see it as a long-term holding for Infratil. "Infratil has two more mature assets in the NZ$1 billion size bracket--Wellington International Airport and Diagnostic Imaging," Forsyth Barr. "Both assets are too small to matter to Infratil now but too big to ignore." Forsyth Barr expects Infratil to consider strategic options for both. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Torque Metals Secures Grant Co-Funding for Drilling at Western Australia Project
data.description:
Torque Metals secured a government co-funded grant worth AU$144,500 under the exploration incentive scheme for diamond drilling at the Paris project in Western Australia, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The drilling aims to explore down-plunge extensions of the gold system beyond the existing Paris deposit mineral resource estimate, the filing said.

Shares fell 2% in afternoon trade Thursday.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei rises; NTT Data set to surge on takeover report
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average edged up on Thursday as chip-related shares tracked a rally in U.S. semiconductor stocks, while NTT Data was set for a 17% jump following a takeover report.

The Nikkei added 0.23% to 36,863.15 by the midday break, after breaking a seven-day winning run on Wednesday.

The broader Topix fell 0.18% to 2,691.36 and was poised to snap a nine-session rising streak.

"The two main indexes have recouped their losses quickly (since U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff announcement last month), but the speed of the recovery has slowed down," said Seiichi Suzuki, chief equity market analyst at Tokai Tokyo Intelligence Laboratory.

"Investors have become cautious as the Nikkei approached the psychological level of 37,000."

Overnight, U.S. stocks rallied close to the closing bell as chipmakers jumped after Bloomberg reported Trump's administration plans to rescind artificial intelligence chip curbs.

A U.S. Commerce Department spokesperson subsequently confirmed the report. The PHLX semiconductor index ended 1.7% higher after losing as much as 1% on the day.

Japan's chip-testing equipment maker Advantest rose 2.82% and chip-making equipment maker Tokyo Electron gained 1.32%.

NTT Data was untraded with a glut of buy orders after the Nikkei newspaper reported telecom giant NTT plans to launch a tender offer worth up to 3 trillion yen ($20.88 billion) to buy the remaining shares in NTT Data.

NTT Data, which provides data networks, was bid at the daily upper limit of 3,492 yen, a 17% jump on Wednesday's close of 2,991.5 yen.

With the Nikkei average futures (JNIc1) crossing the 37,000 level, the bid on NTT Data may continue through the next session, Suzuki said.

Drug maker Torii Pharmaceutical surged 13.62% after peer Shionogi said it would acquire Torii, a subsidiary of Japan Tobacco , for more than 150 billion yen.

Shipping firm Mitsui OSK Lines (9104.) fell 3.2% to become the biggest percentage loser on the Nikkei, followed by technology investor SoftBank Group , which lost 2.36%.

($1 = 143.6600 yen)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Stocks Rise After Fed Holds Rates Steady and U.S. Prepares for China Talks

The Dow industrials add nearly 300 points, pushed higher by a rally in Disney stock.

Trump to Announce Trade Agreement With Britain

The deal is expected to be a framework of an agreement with tariff adjustments.

U.S. to Overhaul Curbs on AI Chip Exports After Industry Backlash

A rule limiting many countries' access to American technology was set to take effect May 15.

BOE Set to Cut Interest Rates Again. Why the Fed Is Lagging Further Behind the U.K.

President Donald Trump's widespread tariffs have increased uncertainty about the outlook across the globe, and BOE officials say that's more likely to bring inflation rates down rather than push them up.

Why the Fed Isn't Ready to Join Other Central Banks in Cutting Rates

The U.S. faces risks of higher inflation and unemployment. Countries without tax hikes on imports are focused on the hit to growth.

Fentanyl Crisis Provided Opening for U.S.-China Trade Talks

A planned meeting in Switzerland will be the first direct talks over trade during Trump's second term.

The Fed's Next Move Depends on Tariffs. It Could Take a While.

President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs have clouded the central bank's economic outlook, leaving it in wait-and-see mode.

Fed Warns of Rising Economic Risks as It Leaves Rates Steady

Officials are puzzling over whether to focus on the risks of higher prices or weaker hiring

Brazil's Central Bank Again Lifts Interest Rates, But Less Aggressively

The bank raised its Selic benchmark to 14.75% from 14.25%, after three consecutive full-point increases.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Fall Amid Lower Production

U.S. crude oil inventories fell by 2 million barrels to 438.4 million barrels in the week ended May 2 and were about 7% below the five-year average for the time of year, the EIA said.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Why Nvidia investors are cheering Trump's likely dismantling of Biden's AI chip rules

The policy change could make it easier for AI chip companies to sell into the Middle East.

Uber Swings to Profit on Bookings Growth

Uber Technologies swung to a first-quarter profit as riders ordered more trips on the digital taxi service, but missed Wall Street revenue estimates.

TPG Looks to Capitalize on Opportunities Driven by Market Dislocation

Private-equity firm says fundraising campaigns will likely take longer, but it still expects to raise more this year than in 2024.

Zillow Group Swings to Profit For First Time Since 2022

CEO said the company's continued expansion of services and scaling of its "housing super app" to reach more real estate markets helped the company's top and bottom lines.

Arm Holdings Posts Higher Sales; Outlook Disappoints

The semiconductor- and software-design company's sales were boosted by the continued adoption of its chips, though its outlook for the current quarter missed expectations.

Applovin Posts Higher Profit, Revenue on Strength in Advertising Business

The tech company's results beat Wall Street's expectations, sending shares sharply higher in after-hours trading.

AI Is Hitting Search Traffic, Testimony Suggests. Google Stock Falls.

An Apple executive testified that Google searches in Apple browsers fell for the first time as people turn to AI for their search queries, according to Bloomberg.

Ford Increases Prices for Certain Vehicles Amid Tariff Uncertainty

The higher prices will apply to 2025 models of the Maverick, Bronco Sport and Mach-E built after May 2.

Barrick Quarterly Earnings Beat Expectations

Record prices for gold and gains for copper buoyed earnings, but an ongoing clash with Mali's military junta cast a shadow over Barrick Mining's stronger-than-expected financial performance.

Teva Will Hike Generic Drug Prices If Trump Imposes Tariffs, CEO Says

The drugmaker reported first-quarter earnings that beat expectations and raised its full-year guidance.

data.title: Chinese Bubble Tea Chain Auntea Jenny's Shares Jump on Hong Kong Debut
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Chinese bubble tea chain Auntea Jenny (Shanghai) Industrial's shares surged on their trading debut in Hong Kong, the latest in a string of offerings from tea sellers.

Shares surged 56% in early trade Thursday to 176.90 Hong Kong dollars. The benchmark Hang Seng Index was 0.8% higher.

Auntea Jenny raised HK$195 million, equivalent to $25.1 million, in net proceeds, which it plans to use on enhancing its digitalization and supply chain capabilities, as well as strengthening research and development, it said in its IPO filing. The company also plans to expand overseas, with Southeast Asian countries as its first choice, as well as North America.

As of the end of 2024, Auntea Jenny had 9,176 stores, almost double from two years ago. The brand mostly sells milk tea with grain toppings and fresh fruit tea priced around $2, targeting consumers in smaller cities, with half of its stores located in China's third- or lower-tier cities.

The tea chain's 2024 revenue dropped 1.9% from the previous year to 3.28 billion yuan, or $ 454 million, while its profit declined 15% to 328.9 million yuan, which it attributed to an overall industry slowdown and intensified competition in China's tea shop industry in 2024.

China's bubble tea market is increasingly crowded and competitive after decades of growth.

Auntea Jenny joins a wave of Chinese bubble tea makers going public this year as they tap capital markets for further expansion. Mixue Group, known for its ice cream and sugary drinks priced under $1, raised $423 million in its IPO in March. Another Chinese tea brand Chagee listed on the Nasdaq last month, raising $411 million.

The size of the global freshly-made beverage market could rise to $1.10 billion in 2028, accounting for nearly half of the global beverage market, First Shanghai Securities analysts said in a research note.

Annual sales in China and Southeast Asia are expected to hit 51 and 36 cups per person, respectively, by 2028, the analysts added. That's more than double 2023 sales.

The listing also comes as IPO activity in Hong Kong heats up. Funds raised from 17 new listings in the city during the first quarter nearly quadrupled to HK$18.7 billion compared with a year earlier.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Geely’s electric vehicle US delisting is shrewd
data.description:
The Chinese group is offering to take $6 bln Zeekr private less than a year after the unit’s New York IPO. Geely is reorganising its sprawling auto businesses to find synergies. But the price of its buyout is opportunistic and leverages US-China financial decoupling fears.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @KatrinaHamlin on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Geely Auto on May 7 offered to take private its New York-listed electric vehicle unit Zeekr Intelligent Technology, valuing the company at $6.5 billion.

The Chinese parent offered to pay $25.66 per Zeekr American Depositary Receipt, a premium of about 14% to Zeekr’s closing price on May 6. Zeekr ADS holders can also opt to receive 12.3 newly issued Geely shares.

Geely owns 65.7% of Zeekr. After the offer was announced, Zeekr shares closed the day's trade up nearly 12% at $25.19.

data.title: NTT Data Shares Indicated Sharply Higher After Parent Considers Taking Full Control
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

NTT Data Group shares were indicated sharply higher after Nippon Telegraph & Telephone said it is considering acquiring shares in the information-technology services subsidiary it doesn't already own.

No shares changed hands due to overwhelming buy orders for NTT Data in Tokyo on Thursday at the limit high of 3,492 yen, a 17% jump.

NTT Data's recent earnings growth has been supported by the strength of its data-center business overseas. The company estimated in February that operating profit would increase 8.5% to Y336.00 billion, equivalent to $2.34 billion, for the fiscal year ended March.

Nippon Telegraph & Telephone said Thursday that its board is set to discuss making NTT Data a wholly owned subsidiary later in the day and that it will make an announcement if and when necessary. NTT Data also said its board will discuss the matter on Thursday. The parent holds a stake of about 58% in the subsidiary. The unit has a market capitalization of about Y4.9 trillion, factoring in Thursday's potential gains.

The companies made the comments after Japanese business daily Nikkei reported that Nippon Telegraph & Telephone planned to turn NTT Data into a wholly owned subsidiary.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: BGSF Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• BGSF Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 7 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, identical to the same quarter last year. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 11 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -18 cents to -4 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 8% to $63.23 million from a year ago; analysts expected $65.25 million.

  • BGSF Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 7 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $722 thousand.

  • BGSF Inc shares had fallen by 8.7% this quarter and lost 35.9% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 46.7% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the employment services peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for BGSF Inc is $10.50

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 03:07 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: New Zealand Total Lending Increases to NZ$12.5 Billion in March
data.description:
New Zealand's total lending increased to NZ$12.5 billion in March from NZ$10.2 billion in February, according to data from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand released on Thursday.

New lending fully secured by residential mortgage jumped to NZ$8.09 billion in the month from with NZ$5.83 in the previous month.

Personal consumer new lending rose to NZ$246 million from NZ$224 million. New lending for business slid to NZ$2.91 billion from NZ$3 billion.

Lending for agriculture purposes soared to NZ$827 million from NZ$512 million. Other new lending fell to NZ$431 million from NZ$637 million.

data.title: UAE-based Aramex Posts Lower Q1 Profit
data.description:
Aramex (DFM:ARMX) said Wednesday its attributable and gross profit in the first quarter dipped year over year.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the parent for the three months ended March 31 was 17.1 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 46.6 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.012 dirham from 0.032 dirham.

The Dubai-listed transportation services company's gross profit was 364.7 million dirhams, compared with 395.4 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Indonesia's FX reserves fall to $152.5 bln at end-April
data.description: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves fell by about $4.6 billion in April to $152.5 billion, enough to cover 6.4 months of imports and above international standards, the central bank said on Thursday.

data.title: Fortinet Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Fortinet Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 58 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 43 cents. The mean expectation of thirty six analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 53 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 52 cents to 60 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 13.8% to $1.54 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.54 billion.

  • Fortinet Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 56 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $433.4 million.

  • Fortinet Inc shares had risen by 11.3% this quarter and gained 13.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 16 "strong buy" or "buy," 26 "hold" and 3 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is also "hold"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Fortinet Inc is $110.12

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 03:08 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Nexus Terminates Dungeon Stalkers Publishing Contract with Hive I.M
data.description:
Nexus terminated the publishing contract of the video game Dungeon Stalkers with Hive I.M upon the latter's request, the South Korean game developer said in a Thursday regulatory filing with Korea Exchange.

The contract was valued at 3 billion won.

The company did not disclose a reason for the termination of the contract.

data.title: Japan futures ease on scepticism over US-China trade talks
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures eased on Thursday, retreating from a three-week high reached in the previous session, as investors remained sceptical that the upcoming U.S.-China trade talks would yield a breakthrough.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for October delivery , was down 0.7 yen, or 0.23%, at 298.3 yen ($2.1) per kg as of 0246 GMT.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery fell 225 yuan, or 1.51%, to 14,645 yuan ($2,024) per metric ton.

  • Traders were focussed on the scheduled U.S.-China trade talks in Switzerland this weekend, after months of escalating tensions that pushed tariffs between the world's two largest economies well beyond 100%. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will meet with China's top economic official.

  • U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday suggested China initiated the trade talks, adding he was not willing to cut U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods to get Beijing to negotiations. Bessent said the upcoming talks are a start, not "advanced" discussions.

  • On Wednesday, the U.S. Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, acknowledging that risks of higher inflation and unemployment had risen, further clouding the U.S. economic outlook amid the effects of Trump's tariffs.

  • Robust demand for hybrids is expected to underpin steady profits at Toyota Motor when the world's top automaker reports annual earnings on Thursday, although investors will be on high alert for any sign of a looming impact from U.S. tariffs.

  • The yen traded at 143.51 against the U.S. dollar, compared with around 143.03 yen in late Wednesday trading in Asia.

  • Japan's Nikkei was up 0.3% on Thursday.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for June delivery last traded at 171.5 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.8%.

($1 = 143.5900 yen)

($1 = 7.2342 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Lendlease Global Commercial REIT's Occupancy Remains Stable in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
Lendlease Global Commercial REIT's committed portfolio occupancy remained stable at 92.1% as at March 31, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The REIT's tenant retention rate stood at 87.9% while rental reversion came in at 10.4% in fiscal Q3.

During the period, the REIT was also awarded a tender to redevelop a car park at Grange Road, Singapore.

The REIT also issued SG$120 million perpetual bonds at 4.75% per year to refinance SG$200 million 5.25% perpetual bonds due in April 2025.

data.title: Indonesia Forex Reserves Fall to 5-Month Low
data.description:
Indonesia’s foreign exchange reserves fell to USD 152.5 billion in April 2025, down from USD 157.1 billion in March — the highest level on record.

This marks the lowest level since last November, primarily due to the government’s external debt payments and the central bank’s ongoing efforts to stabilize the rupiah amid persistent global financial market uncertainty.

The latest figures are sufficient to cover 6.4 months of imports or 6.2 months of imports and government foreign debt payments, remaining well above the international adequacy standard of around three months of imports.

The central bank reaffirmed that foreign exchange reserves are expected to remain adequate in the coming months, supported by a positive export outlook, a surplus in the capital and financial account, and attractive investment returns.

data.title: Moody's Revises Outlook on Volvo Cars to Negative Amid Challenging Market Environment
data.description:
Moody's on Wednesday downgraded its outlook on Volvo Car (VOLCAR-B.ST), d/b/a Volvo Cars, to negative from stable, citing concerns for the company's operations amid challenging market conditions.

The rating agency said it may decide to lower the Swedish carmaker's affirmed Ba1 corporate family rating to Ba2 in the absence of operational recovery in the coming months.

data.title: Vital Healthcare Property Trust's AFFO Falls in Nine Months Ended March
data.description:
Vital Healthcare Property Trust reported Thursday that its adjusted funds from operations (AFFO) per security in the nine months to March 31 fell to NZ$7.78, compared with the prior year's NZ$8.24.

Net property income for the quarter ended March 31 was NZ$37 million, compared with NZ$36 million a year earlier.

The board also declared a quarterly distribution of NZ$0.024375 per share, unchanged from NZ$0.024375 in the previously reported period, payable June 20 to shareholders on record as of June 5.

The company also reaffirmed its fiscal year distribution guidance of NZ$0.0975 per unit.

data.title: Budweiser APAC's Q1 Profit Slides; EPS, Revenue Miss Estimates
data.description:
Budweiser Brewing APAC's attributable profit fell to $234 million in the first quarter from $287 million in the year-ago period, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Basic earnings per share at the brewery decreased to $0.0178 from $0.0226 in the year-ago period. Visible Alpha analysts estimated EPS at $0.02.

Revenue declined 7.5% to $1.46 billion from $1.64 billion in the prior-year period. Analysts at Visible Alpha forecast revenue at $1.52 billion.

Shares slid 1% during morning trading on Thursday.

data.title: A Saudi Arabian producer offers Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa informed that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have offered its PVC resin Suspension (67S) grade at the USD 900/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa reported that a Saudi Arabian producer is heard to have announced its offer for PP Impact Copolymer (412MK49/312MK10/312MK40/48MK40/513MN40) grades at the USD 1120-1130/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: Market Chatter: Tesla's China-Made EV Sales Drop 6% Amid Fierce Competition, Reputational Damage
data.description:
Tesla's China-made vehicle sales continued to fall for the seventh straight month, with April sales slumping 6% year on year, amid fierce competition from Chinese rivals and reputational damage in Europe, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Data from the China Passenger Car Association showed that April deliveries of Model 3 and Model Y vehicles dropped 26% month on month to 58,459 units, according to the report.

Tesla's European sales also plunged in April as Chief executive Elon Musk's far-right political endorsements alienated buyers, boosting Chinese automakers' market share. Protests against Musk in the US have further impacted Tesla's sales, Reuters wrote.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Australian Dollar Rebounds on Global Trade Optimism
data.description:
The Australian dollar climbed above $0.645 on Thursday, recovering roughly half of the previous session’s losses as optimism surrounding global trade developments lifted sentiment.

Support for the Aussie came after President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social that he would hold a press conference Thursday to announce a deal with a “big” country, reportedly the United Kingdom.

Additionally, top U.S. officials are scheduled to meet their Chinese counterparts in Switzerland this weekend in a fresh bid to ease trade tensions.

Still, Trump maintained that he would not reduce tariffs on China as a condition to begin formal negotiations.

Elsewhere, the Federal Reserve kept rates unchanged and signaled a cautious stance, highlighting uncertainty linked to tariffs.

On the domestic front, markets are increasingly pricing in a 25 basis point rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia at its May meeting, as signs of a slowing economy and growing external headwinds weigh on the outlook.
